behavior_summary: U8P_CHAIR::look is the object's 'U8P_CHAIR::look/examine' handler: when the player examines the chair it presents the short description 'wooden chair' and then suspends/returns control to the engine.

U8P_CHAIR::gotHit handles the object being struck. If its first parameter (param1) is false/zero (inferred condition: 'not param1'), the routine calls Item::setType(0x022A, this) to change the in-game item type/identity of this object — likely flipping the chair into a different state (e.g., broken or alternate object).
No global flags are read or written by these routines, and there is no player menu/dialogue branching present.
notable_control_flow: U8P_CHAIR::look: simple linear flow — open block, emit a single bark ('wooden chair'), then suspend/end the U8P_CHAIR::look process.
U8P_CHAIR::gotHit: conditional flow — test first parameter (param1); when the test is false/zero, call external Item::setType to change this item's type to 0x022A; otherwise do nothing.
Both functions are implemented as engine processes (execution form: process); U8P_CHAIR::gotHit is a helper process invoked by engine damage/hit events.
open_questions: What concrete gameplay state does item type 0x022A represent (broken chair, alternate object, removed/converted state)? — inferred but not certain.
What exact semantics does the first parameter (param1) to U8P_CHAIR::gotHit carry (boolean 'fatal hit', source mask, actor id)? — usage suggests a boolean-like guard but confidence is low.
When and by which engine code paths is U8P_CHAIR::gotHit invoked (player attack, environment damage, thrown object)? — not present in snippet.
Is the second parameter to U8P_CHAIR::gotHit used elsewhere or intentionally unused in this object's logic?

main_function.name: U8P_CHAIR::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description displayed when the player examines the chair (bark: 'wooden chair').
entry_conditions: Triggered by the engine when the player (or relevant actor) performs a 'U8P_CHAIR::look'/'examine' action on this object.
Also may be triggered by any engine fast-area U8P_CHAIR::look/examine entry semantics for objects of this type.

function_map.name: U8P_CHAIR::look
function_map.role: object U8P_CHAIR::look/examine handler that emits a short description to the player

function_map.name: U8P_CHAIR::gotHit
function_map.role: hit/damage handler that conditionally changes the item's type/state

function_map.name: Item::setType
function_map.role: engine call to change an item's type/identity (used by U8P_CHAIR::gotHit to flip this object to 0x022A)

external_calls.purpose: Change this item's type/identity to 0x022A, effecting a state transition for the chair (inferred: broken/converted state).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_CHAIR::look is the object's 'U8P_CHAIR::look/examine' handler: when the player examines the chair it presents the short description 'wooden chair' and then suspends/returns control to the engine.
